title: Superabsorbent polymeric materials produced from de-chlorophyllized green plant material
abstract: A method for producing a superabsorbent material from a synthetic polymer and a water- soluble protein concentrate derived plant biomass is disclosed, comprising: preparing an aqueous mixture of a copolymer comprising carboxylic groups; preparing an aqueous solution of an inorganic salt; preparing an aqueous mixture of a water-soluble protein concentrate derived from green plant biomass; mixing said aqueous mixture of said synthetic polymer with said inorganic salt solution to form synthetic polymer-inorganic salt mixture; adding said aqueous mixture of said water-soluble protein concentrate to said synthetic polymer-inorganic salt mixture to form an aqueous mixture of polymeric material; drying said polymeric material; and thermally crosslinking said polymeric material to form a superabsorbent swellable polymeric material. Also disclosed is the superabsorbent material produced by the method.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS We claim: 1. A method for preparing a superabsorbent swellable polymeric material, comprising: preparing an aqueous mixture of a synthetic polymer comprising carboxylic groups; preparing an aqueous solution of an inorganic salt; preparing an aqueous mixture of a water-soluble protein concentrate derived from green plant biomass; mixing said aqueous mixture of said synthetic polymer with said inorganic salt solution to form a synthetic polymer-inorganic salt mixture; adding said aqueous mixture of said water-soluble protein concentrate to said synthetic polymer-inorganic salt mixture to form an aqueous mixture of polymeric material; drying said polymeric material; and, thermally crosslinking said polymeric material to form a superabsorbent swellable polymeric material. 2. The method according to claim 1, wherein said step of producing an aqueous mixture of water-soluble protein concentrate from plant biomass comprises: obtaining dry de-chlorophyllized plant matter from said plant biomass; treating said dry de-chlorophyllized plant matter with water, thereby at least partially dissolving water-soluble protein content of said dry de-chlorophyllized plant matter and preparing an aqueous suspension of said dry de-chlorophyllized plant matter; separating said aqueous suspension of said dry de-chlorophyllized plant matter into a first neutral extract and a wet solid; and, drying said first neutral extract, thereby yielding a water-soluble protein concentrate. 3. The method according to claim 2, wherein said step of producing an aqueous mixture of water-soluble protein concentrate from plant biomass comprises: washing said wet solid with water, thereby producing a second neutral extract; concentrating said second neutral extract; and, drying said second neutral extract, thereby yielding additional water-soluble protein concentrate. 4. The method according to claim 2, wherein said step of producing an aqueous mixture of water-soluble protein concentrate from plant biomass comprises: washing said wet solid with water, thereby producing a second neutral extract; and, 

 combining said first neutral extract and said second neutral extract prior to said step of drying said first neutral extract, thereby producing a combined neutral extract; wherein said step of drying said first neutral extract comprises drying said combined neutral extract, thereby yielding a water-soluble protein concentrate. 5. The method according to claim 1, wherein said plant biomass is obtained from aquatic plants. 6. The method according to claim 1, wherein said plant biomass is obtained from duckweed. 7. The method according to claim 1, wherein said plant biomass does not comprise seeds. 8. The method according to claim 1, wherein said method does not comprise any step in which said plant biomass contacts a solvent that is not approved for use in food production. 9. The method according to claim 1, wherein said method does not comprise any step in which a chemical lysis agent is used. 10. The method according to claim 2, wherein said step of obtaining dry de-chlorophyllized plant matter comprises: drying said plant biomass, thereby producing dried plant biomass; grinding said dried plant biomass, thereby producing ground dried plant biomass; extracting chlorophyll from said ground dried plant biomass, thereby producing de- chlorophyllized plant matter; and, drying said de-chlorophyllized plant matter, thereby obtaining dry de-chlorophyllized plant matter. 11. The method according to claim 2, wherein said step of grinding said dried plant biomass comprises grinding said dried plant biomass to a powder characterized by a maximum particle diameter of 100 Î¼Î¹Î·. 12. The method according to claim 2, wherein said step of treating said dry de-chlorophyllized plant matter with water comprises at least one step selected from the group consisting of: treating for 4 - 6 hours; treating dry de-chlorophyllized plant matter with water in a plant matter/water ratio selected from the group consisting of: 5:95 by weight on a dry matter basis; 10:90 by weight on a dry matter basis; and, 20:80 by weight on a dry matter basis; and, 

 treating dry de-chlorophyllized plant matter with water at a temperature in a range selected from the group consisting of: 20 - 80 Â°C; 30 - 70 Â°C; and, 40 - 60 Â°C. 13. The method according to claim 2, wherein said first neutral extract produced in said step of separating said aqueous suspension of said dry de-chlorophyllized plant matter into a first neutral extract and a wet solid comprises a solid content in a range selected from the group consisting of: 5 - 10% by weight; 1 - 3% by weight; and, 0.5 - 2.5% by weight. 14. The method according to claim 2, wherein said step of washing said wet solid with water comprises at least one step selected from the group consisting of: washing said wet solid with water at a solid : water ratio of 1 : 3 by volume; washing at a temperature of 10 Â°C; and, washing with successive aliquots of water until an aliquot is produced that is characterized by a concentration of dissolved material of less than 0.1% by weight. 15. The method according to claim 2, wherein said step of concentrating said second neutral extract comprises concentrating said second neutral extract until said neutral extract is characterized by a dissolved solid content of not less than 3%. 16. The method according to either one of claims 2 or 3, comprising drying said wet solid after all steps of producing neutral extracts have been completed, thereby producing dry fibrous material. 17. The method according to claim 16, wherein said step of drying said wet solid comprises drying said wet solid until said wet solid is characterized by a moisture content of less than 15%. 18. The method according to claim 16, wherein said step of drying said wet solid is followed by a step of grinding said dry fibrous material. 19. A superabsorbent swellable polymeric material, comprising a composite of a water-soluble protein concentrate derived from de-chlorophyllized green plant biomass and a copolymer comprising carboxyl groups. 0. The superabsorbent swellable polymeric material according to claim 19, produced by the method according to any one of claims 1 - 3.
